commitMessage:
Change String compare from == to .equals

Change-Id: If3989918908d31865353829d87acf43bbb8e248a
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
categoryReason: Modernizes comparison logic for objects and strings for null safety and correctness.
summary: This commit modernizes various `==` and `!=` comparisons for String and Object types to use `java.util.Objects.equals()` for improved null safety and correctness. It also converts existing `.equals()` calls to `Objects.equals()` for consistency. The transformation is applicable to Java 7+ environments (baseline 8) and is fully expressible as a set of GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules. The rules differentiate between String comparisons (where `==` is often a bug) and general Object comparisons, as well as converting existing `.equals()` calls. The rules specifically target non-primitive types by using `instanceof("java.lang.Object")`.

commitMessage:
Bug 561481 - Improve text for non-blocking preference of JDT

Change-Id: I9c75bccb6fd720e27d1d1d10beb8155f2016088e
Signed-off-by: Lars Vogel <Lars.Vogel@vogella.com>
irrelevantReason: This commit only modifies a `.properties` file (resource bundle text). It is not a Java code transformation and thus irrelevant for a Java code transformation DSL.
summary: The commit changes text in a `PreferencesMessages.properties` file. This is not a Java code change and therefore not relevant for a Java code transformation DSL. It falls outside the scope of pattern-based code refactoring.

commitMessage:
Bug 548309 lift java version from 1.4 to 1.8

Change-Id: I037e7712dccdb3876dae22e69383749ab2c64705
Signed-off-by: Fabrice Tiercelin <fabrice.tiercelin@yahoo.fr>
categoryReason: Modernizes Java code by converting anonymous inner classes to lambdas/method references and optimizing lambda syntax, introduced in Java 8.
summary: This commit extensively modernizes Java code to leverage Java 8 features. It replaces anonymous inner `Comparator` implementations with `Comparator.comparing()` using lambdas or method references. It also converts single-argument lambda expressions (`(arg) -> method(arg)`) and zero-argument lambdas (`() -> method()`) to method references (`Class::method`). A cosmetic change involves removing parentheses for single-argument lambdas. All these patterns are direct 1:1 replacements, semantically safe, and fully implementable as GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules, applicable for Java 8 and higher projects.
dslValidationResult: Parse error at line 2: Expected '=>' or ';;' but found: @java.lang.Override (line 2)

commitMessage:
Bug 559906 - improved ResourceWorkingSetFilter performance

Don't iterate over all java projects in workspace when checking working
set containment.
Instead only try to create a JavaElement for the resource's project.

Change-Id: I4034fa13d975142273e28f30fd4d242f5fa68a76
Signed-off-by: Julian Honnen <julian.honnen@vector.com>
categoryReason: The commit performs a significant architectural refactoring within JDT internal code, including removing a field, replacing an if-block with a new helper method, and adding the helper method.
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL would need to support:
1. Pattern matching and replacement of entire `if` statements or sequences of statements.
2. Adding new method declarations.
3. Removing field declarations from a class body.
summary: This commit refactors internal JDT UI code to improve performance in `ResourceWorkingSetFilter`. It removes a field, replaces an `if` block with a new helper method call, and introduces that new helper method. These are structural changes (field removal, if-to-method call, method addition) that cannot be expressed by the current TriggerPattern DSL, thus classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 558893 - Make completion run in non-UI Thread by default

Change-Id: I8918505265eb4576493650e596c2409aad744097
Signed-off-by: Mickael Istria <mistria@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The change directly modifies the default value of an Eclipse preference constant, which is a specific form of API configuration.
summary: This commit changes the default value of the `CODEASSIST_NONUITHREAD_COMPUTATION` preference from `false` to `true` in `PreferenceConstants.java`. This is a direct 1:1 replacement of a boolean literal within a method call, which is fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL. This is a GREEN transformation.

commitMessage:
Bug 561238 - [14] User incorrectly suggested to use an unavailable
release version

Change-Id: Ifb0d5b2626c07467834073609a3b70dfb74c6958
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: The commit primarily fixes a typo in an internal JDT field name and all its usages.
summary: This commit fixes a typo in the `fComplierReleaseCheck` field name to `fCompilerReleaseCheck` across its declaration and all usages in `ComplianceConfigurationBlock.java`. The TriggerPattern DSL can directly replace the identifier for this field wherever it is used within the class. This is a simple 1:1 identifier replacement, which is fully supported and classified as GREEN.

commitMessage:
Bug 560150 - Restore default doesn't work for Use '--release' option

Change-Id: I025a20bf0e7cab716d0023418279bfc590f08c2e
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: The rule specifically addresses a configuration issue within Eclipse JDT UI preferences, which aligns with API configuration.
summary: This commit fixes a bug (Bug 560150) in JDT's `ComplianceConfigurationBlock` where the 'Use --release' option's default value was not correctly restored when performing defaults. It replaces a hardcoded `DISABLED` constant as an argument to `setDefaultValue()` with a ternary expression that dynamically retrieves the actual default value from the `complianceOptions` map, falling back to `DISABLED` if `null`. This ensures the preference's default behavior is properly maintained. This is a direct 1:1 expression replacement of a method argument, making it fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. Other changes involve adding new methods and overriding `performDefaults()`, which are structural changes not expressible by the DSL.

commitMessage:
Change String compare from == to .equals

Change-Id: Ieafc86ab65bf8c0e51357cd24692d7a984b5b6a2
Depends-On: If3989918908d31865353829d87acf43bbb8e248a
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
categoryReason: Introduces null-safe equality and inequality checks using java.util.Objects.equals() to replace verbose manual null checks.
summary: The commit replaces verbose null-safe object equality comparisons (e.g., `(A == null && B == null) || (A != null && A.equals(B))`) with the more concise and readable `java.util.Objects.equals(A, B)`, and its negative form (`!Objects.equals(A, B)`). This is a standard Java modernization pattern to improve null safety and readability, as `Objects.equals()` (introduced in Java 7) handles `null` arguments gracefully. The transformations are direct 1:1 expression replacements and are fully implementable as two GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules. The existing `CPListElementAttribute.java` change is a structural simplification using already present `Objects.equals` calls, which is out of scope for this specific pattern.

commitMessage:
Bug 561483 - [postfix] Handle method invocations containing blocks

- Handle postfix completion on anonymous method declarations
- Format PostfixCompletionTests to use 2-space tabs in order to make
  generating examples easier

Change-Id: I30e1438deef7b4286a6a6d17b3e5a041ff35663e
irrelevantReason: Adds new logic to an internal JDT postfix completion AST visitor. This is an IDE feature implementation (AST analysis for a specific UI function) rather than a code transformation pattern for existing user code.
categoryReason: The change implements internal IDE feature logic (postfix completion detection) using AST traversal, not a general code transformation or refactoring pattern.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support defining custom AST visitors, complex logic involving selection offsets, method binding properties (beyond simple type checks), or internal state management for IDE features.
summary: This commit adds logic to an internal JDT UI AST visitor for postfix completion, specifically detecting method invocations based on return type and cursor position. This is an IDE feature implementation (AST analysis) and cannot be generalized into a TriggerPattern DSL rule for code transformations.

commitMessage:
Bug 561379 - [clean up] lambda cleanup wrongly removes anonymous
subclass

Change-Id: I57e72f1fa6d03154d8eda8305afa112f9df3c073
Signed-off-by: Fabrice Tiercelin <fabrice.tiercelin@yahoo.fr>
categoryReason: The change is a bug fix within an existing JDT cleanup (LambdaExpressionAndMethodRefCleanUp.java), not a new generalizable code transformation pattern.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support modifying the internal logic of an existing Java-based cleanup. Specifically, it cannot add complex conditional checks (e.g., ci.getAnonymousClassDeclaration() == null) to an existing cleanup's application logic.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in an existing JDT lambda cleanup. The cleanup was incorrectly converting anonymous subclasses to lambdas. The fix adds a new condition to the cleanup's internal logic (`ci.getAnonymousClassDeclaration() == null`) to prevent this erroneous transformation. This is a bug fix within an existing Java cleanup implementation and cannot be expressed as a pattern-based DSL rule. It would require modifying the Java code of the cleanup itself.

commitMessage:
Bug 561641 - [14] [javadoc] Proposal for missing javadoc on Record does
not generate tags for record components

Change-Id: Icc1aabc61076c33f7e478138184ade6c6f5efdeb
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: This category specifically targets enhancements to Javadoc generation logic, distinct from general code refactorings or simple documentation changes.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs capabilities for dynamic string construction (e.g., multi-line Javadoc comments with custom tags), conditional logic based on AST node types (e.g., instanceof RecordDeclaration), and iterating over lists of AST nodes to extract information (e.g., record components for @param tags). These complex logic and dynamic content generation features are not supported by the current expression-based DSL.
summary: This commit enhances JDT's quick assist/fix for generating Javadoc. It adds specific logic to recognize `RecordDeclaration` (Java 14+) and extract its `recordComponents()` to generate `@param` tags for them. This involves complex AST traversal, conditional logic based on node types, and dynamic string generation for Javadoc comments, which are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL. It is classified as RED because of these advanced requirements.

commitMessage:
Bug 561670 - [cleanup & saveaction] Clean lambda expression into this
method reference

Change-Id: Ibbe15ea4461db349fd27b8a3f1ef5a1105ded57e
Signed-off-by: Fabrice Tiercelin <fabrice.tiercelin@yahoo.fr>
categoryReason: The commit modifies the internal decision-making logic of an existing cleanup rather than introducing a new code transformation pattern. This is an enhancement to how the cleanup identifies valid transformation targets.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would require more sophisticated, binding-aware guards capable of performing type hierarchy analysis (e.g., `isSuperType(type1, type2)`) and complex conditional logic based on placeholder properties (e.g., checking if `calledExpression == null` and then performing further analysis). The current DSL's custom guard functions from embedded Java blocks are stubs that always return true, so they cannot execute complex logic like `Bindings.isSuperType` to dynamically decide the applicability of a rule.
summary: This commit refines the internal logic of the `LambdaExpressionAndMethodRefCleanUp` in JDT UI. It adds a new conditional check to determine when a lambda expression can be converted into a method reference (specifically for cases where the target expression is implicitly `this`). The new logic involves checking if `calledExpression` is null and then performing type binding comparisons (`Bindings.isSuperType`) between the method's declaring class and the enclosing type. This is an internal enhancement to an existing Java-based cleanup's decision tree, making it more intelligent about when to apply a transformation. The complex, binding-dependent conditional logic is not directly expressible using the current TriggerPattern DSL's pattern matching or guards, classifying it as a RED transformation.

commitMessage:
Bug 561483 - Allow proper handling of void method invocations

- Also handle super method invocations as they do not get handled by the
standard method invocation visit method

Change-Id: I872cf6259f5b7f8be417643bf7040fdc8a734bcf
irrelevantReason: Internal refactoring of an Eclipse JDT AST visitor logic (PostfixCompletionProposalComputer) to handle MethodInvocation and SuperMethodInvocation uniformly as Expressions. This is an internal framework change, not a transformation pattern for end-user code.
categoryReason: The commit refactors how JDT's internal completion proposal computer processes AST nodes, specifically handling method invocations. This falls under internal architectural changes of the JDT framework.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support defining or modifying internal AST visitor logic.
summary: This commit refactors JDT's internal `PostfixCompletionProposalComputer` to unify handling of `MethodInvocation` and `SuperMethodInvocation` by delegating to a common `visit(Expression)` method. It also removes a specific check for `void` methods. This is an internal architectural change within JDT and does not represent a reusable code transformation pattern for end-user Java code, making it not applicable for TriggerPattern DSL rule generation.

partition type.

It adds a conditional check to disable smart insertion of braces/semicolons in non-default partitions (e.g., string literals). This is a change to the IDE's behavior, not a code transformation pattern for end-user Java code.

summary: This commit modifies JDT's internal `SmartSemicolonAutoEditStrategy` to prevent smart insertion of braces/semicolons when inside string literals. This is an internal change to the Eclipse IDE's editing behavior, not a transformation pattern for user code, therefore it is not applicable for TriggerPattern DSL rule generation.

commitMessage:
Bug 551600 - [AutoRefactor immigration #12/117] [cleanup & saveaction]
Use lazy logical operator

Change-Id: I97293585d5ebc352e5879985148cdd580112ee5e
Signed-off-by: Fabrice-TIERCELIN <fabrice.tiercelin@yahoo.fr>
irrelevantReason: The commit adds a UI configuration line (a method call to `intent()`) within an Eclipse JDT preference page. This is a change to the JDT's own UI and configuration, not a transformation pattern applicable to general Java code that the DSL is designed to detect and rewrite.

commitMessage:
Bug 561594 - [chain] Enable chain completion for category cycling

Change-Id: I7b1947cde62d943a41845655101b6618b34ed5ff
categoryReason: The change pertains to an internal Eclipse JDT UI preference constant.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need advanced string manipulation capabilities (e.g., regex with capture groups and replacement logic, or data structure-aware parsing for delimited strings) to abstractly represent reordering and renumbering elements within a complex string literal. A simple 1:1 string literal replacement would be too brittle to be considered a reusable pattern.
summary: The commit modifies a highly specific, hardcoded string literal that defines the order of code assist categories in JDT UI preferences. The change involves reordering a particular category ('javaChainProposalCategory') and updating its associated numeric order. While the DSL can perform exact string literal replacements, this particular change is not a generalizable code transformation pattern. A DSL rule matching and replacing this entire string literal would be extremely brittle, breaking with any minor change to other categories or their order. This is effectively an update of internal configuration data embedded in code rather than a reusable structural or API transformation. Therefore, it is classified as RED because it's not suitable for pattern-based transformations with the current DSL capabilities for string manipulation.

commitMessage:
Reduce number of warnings in build

Although not wrong the current way to use @SuppressWarnings("nls") on
method level instead of using //$NON-NLS-1$ on string constants causes
warnings in the log:
At least one of the problems in category 'nls' is not analysed due to a
compiler option being ignored
Maybe it is an option to switch to using "$NON-NLS-1$" to reduce
warnings to make it easier not to overlook an important warning.

Change-Id: I965403a43369cb4e164f9295ca1941d1a9f9d0f5
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
categoryReason: The change is primarily a code style improvement related to managing NLS warnings.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need explicit support for inserting or manipulating comment nodes (like //$NON-NLS-1$ markers) and for intelligent removal of @SuppressWarnings annotations based on the presence of such markers, which goes beyond simple expression rewriting.
summary: This commit aims to reduce NLS warnings by replacing method-level `@SuppressWarnings("nls")` annotations with inline `//$NON-NLS-1$` comments within string literals in various `getPreview()` methods of JDT CleanUp classes. While this is a code style improvement, implementing it robustly in the current TriggerPattern DSL is challenging. The DSL cannot perform the semantic analysis required to determine if a `@SuppressWarnings("nls")` is truly redundant based on new inline comments, nor does it have direct support for inserting comments into string literals. The DSL explicitly states that comments are stripped before rule parsing. Thus, it's classified as YELLOW, indicating it would require significant DSL extensions for comment manipulation and contextual annotation removal.

commitMessage:
Clean-up: remove unnecessary, non-externalized exception message

Change-Id: I3a2a03d6307b061e7607c5278700a09c21ec4a2f
categoryReason: The change is a very specific internal cleanup within a JDT build path configuration class.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs the ability to: 1. Remove a specific argument (string literal) from a constructor call. 2. Insert a new line comment before an existing statement. This requires advanced AST manipulation beyond simple expression replacement.
summary: This commit removes a specific string literal argument from an `UnsupportedOperationException` constructor call (`"should be handled specifically"`) and inserts a line comment (`// we should not get here...`) before the `throw` statement. This combined transformation of removing a specific argument and adding a new line comment in a different AST position is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL. Comments are generally stripped during parsing and cannot be explicitly inserted as part of a rule replacement. This is a highly specific internal cleanup and thus classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Avoid unnecessary array creation for varargs in o.e.jdt.ui

This one uses the JDT cleanup action:
* Remove unnecessary array creation for varargs

Change-Id: I9924366ba5284b46160f6468f4f037c38270c3e4
Signed-off-by: Lars Vogel <Lars.Vogel@vogella.com>
categoryReason: This pattern reduces unnecessary object allocations by removing redundant array creations in varargs contexts, which is a performance improvement.
summary: This commit systematically removes redundant array creations (`new Type[] { ... }`) when used as varargs arguments to methods and constructors. This is a common performance and code style cleanup, as the array is implicitly created by the Java compiler for varargs. The patterns include `Class.getConstructor(...)`, `Constructor.newInstance(...)`, `Arrays.asList(...)`, `MessageFormat.format(...)`, `setInitialSelections(...)` and `Combo.setItems(...)`. Each specific replacement is a direct 1:1 expression transformation and can be fully automated by the TriggerPattern DSL. Since varargs were introduced in Java 5, no explicit `sourceVersionGE` guard is needed beyond the Java 8 baseline. The `dslRule` field contains multiple rules to cover the different contexts found in the diff.

commitMessage:
Bug 561986 - [chain] Fix proposal inserted with no explicit invocation

Currently, a range of (invocationOffset, invocationOffset + tokenLength)
is used for defining the tokens to be replaced when a chain completion
proposal is applied. This range fails for completions that don't use an
explicit invocation trigger character. Instead, use a range of
(tokenStart, tokenStart + tokenLength) to correctly define the tokens to
be replaced.

- Add testcase

Change-Id: Ic403df42f0750de05b39477acf055b6afb42fb1b
categoryReason: This commit fixes a bug in JDT's internal content assist mechanism by correcting the range calculation for chain completion proposals.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs the ability to introduce new local variable declarations as part of a replacement pattern for an expression, not just a full statement replacement. For example, `expr_A => { Type var = expr_B; expr_C(var); }` where `expr_A` is part of a larger expression context.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in JDT's internal `ChainCompletionTemplateBuilder` related to incorrect token range calculation for content assist. The core change is replacing `contentAssistContext.getInvocationOffset()` with `ctx.getTokenStart()` where `ctx` is a newly introduced local variable (`final CompletionContext ctx = contentAssistContext.getCoreContext();`). The TriggerPattern DSL, in its current form, supports replacing expressions or multi-line statement blocks, but it does not support introducing a new local variable declaration (a statement) immediately before an expression substitution within another expression (e.g., inside a constructor's argument list). This makes the transformation fall into the RED category, as it requires a more advanced DSL capability for introducing intermediate variables.

commitMessage:
Bug 531893 - Replace usage of IDE#getEditorDescriptor(IFile)

Using allowInteractive=false to emulate the previous behavior.

Change-Id: I3bc797fc888815b11f681b5a128b28b433ae34f2
Signed-off-by: Karsten Thoms <karsten.thoms@karakun.com>
categoryReason: This commit updates usage of a deprecated Eclipse API (IDE.getEditorDescriptor) to its new overload with additional arguments.
summary: This commit modernizes the usage of `org.eclipse.ui.ide.IDE.getEditorDescriptor()` by replacing calls to its deprecated overloads (taking `IFile` or `String` name) with a new 3-argument overload. The new method signature includes `boolean allowUnassociated` and `boolean allowInteractive`, for which `true` and `false` are consistently passed, respectively. This is a direct 1:1 method call replacement with added literal arguments, which is fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL. Two separate rules are provided to handle the `IFile` and `String` argument forms of the original method. This is a GREEN transformation, improving Eclipse API adherence. No specific `sourceVersionGE` guard is needed as this is an Eclipse API change, not directly tied to a new Java language feature beyond the Java 8 baseline.

commitMessage:
Bug 560007 - [14][javadoc] Records - javadoc support for records in jdt
ui

Change-Id: I86928dba5d3cbef3a49728b6c8bbb7c05c49ff51
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: Adds internal JDT UI support for a new Java language feature (Records) in Javadoc generation.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs capabilities for adding new methods, complex control flow, and conditional code generation based on AST node types. Specifically, it would require generating `if-else if` structures to differentiate between `MethodDeclaration`, `TypeDeclaration`, and `RecordDeclaration` for handling parameters and type parameters, and to manage method calls like `parameters()` vs `recordComponents()`.
summary: This commit adds Javadoc support for Java 14 Records within the internal JDT UI correction processor (`JavadocTagsSubProcessor.java`). This involves significant structural changes, including adding new `if-else if` blocks, introducing a new private helper method (`insertAllMissingRecordTypeTags`), and modifying existing complex conditional logic to incorporate `RecordDeclaration` handling. These types of complex structural modifications, method additions, and control flow changes are beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based transformations, hence it is classified as RED. The `minJavaVersion` for the hint file would be 14 due to the introduction of Records.

commitMessage:
fix warning

Change-Id: I54a1b0c382dcb47301689eec900e7b7c529352e9
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
categoryReason: Modernizes local variable declarations by replacing generic `Object` types with more specific, inferred types to resolve raw type warnings and improve type safety.
summary: This commit fixes compiler warnings in `DefaultClasspathFixProcessor.java` by replacing `Object` as the declared type for local variables with more specific types (`TypeNameMatch` and `String`). This is a direct type replacement for local variable declarations. The transformation improves type safety and removes raw type warnings. The TriggerPattern DSL can directly implement this change by matching the `Object` declaration and replacing the type, guarded by an `instanceof` check to confirm the initializer's actual type, ensuring semantic correctness. No specific `sourceVersionGE` guard is required as this is a general Java type safety improvement and applies from the Java 8 baseline.

commitMessage:
Simplify lambda expression and method reference syntax

Change-Id: Iac4f43ec28e724777ea3c12fa015cb9621a48b6d
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
summary: This commit simplifies a lambda expression `p -> p.getName()` to a method reference `IProject::getName()` within a Stream API chain in `BuildpathProblemMarkerResolutionGenerator.java`. This is a standard modernization pattern that improves code readability and conciseness. The TriggerPattern DSL can directly implement this by matching the lambda pattern and replacing it with the method reference. An `instanceof` guard for the lambda parameter `$p` ensures that the transformation is type-safe and applies only when `$p` is an `IProject`. This pattern is a GREEN transformation, suitable for a new `lambda-simplification.sandbox-hint` file. It applies to Java 8 and higher, as lambdas and streams were introduced in Java 8.

commitMessage:
Bug 561798 - don't exit linked mode on semicolon in string literal

Change-Id: I3a1159d42dce743dacfbad0ca000d4793717b966
Signed-off-by: Julian Honnen <julian.honnen@vector.com>
categoryReason: Fixes an internal UI bug related to text editing and linked mode by adjusting exception handling and method signatures.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need support for wrapping existing statements in a `try-catch` block, modifying method signatures to add `throws` clauses, and moving blocks of code conditionally. These are structural changes beyond expression-level replacement.
summary: This commit fixes an internal bug (Bug 561798) in JDT's text correction (`ParameterGuessingProposal.java`) to prevent exiting linked mode prematurely in string literals when a semicolon is typed. The changes involve complex structural modifications: wrapping a significant portion of the `doExit` method's logic in a new `try-catch (BadLocationException e)` block, moving an existing semicolon-handling `if` block into this new `try` block, and changing the signature of `canExitLinkedMode` to declare `throws BadLocationException`. These are complex structural changes related to control flow, exception handling, and method signatures, which are beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, it is classified as RED. It applies to Java 8 baseline.

commitMessage:
Bug 489350 - [common navigator] Double click on Java class in Project
Explorer expands class

Change-Id: I6b94e8fbcf5bfd6409ab97272b3ff49a9189aacf
Signed-off-by: Paul Pazderski <paul-eclipse@ppazderski.de>
categoryReason: The changes are specific to internal Eclipse JDT UI components and logic for handling user interactions in the Package Explorer, focusing on refactoring control flow and internal field types rather than general Java API modernization.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need capabilities for: 1. Changing field types (e.g., `private IAction fOpenAndExpand;` to `private OpenAndExpand fOpenAndExpand;`). 2. Complex control flow restructuring, including adding new `if` blocks, moving statements between `if`/`else` branches, and inserting new method calls within conditional blocks. 3. Automatically adding braces to single-statement `if`/`else` blocks.
summary: This commit fixes a UI bug by refactoring internal Eclipse JDT UI code, primarily involving structural changes to `if-else` blocks, adding null checks, and updating a field's declared type. These changes are too complex and structural for the current TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on expression-level replacements. The field type change and extensive control flow modifications cannot be expressed as simple pattern-based rules.

commitMessage:
Unnecessary temporary during conversion from String

Boxing types have parseXXX methods, which perform the conversion without
creating the temporary instance.

Change-Id: Ie5df496d8ee947ead9e0a02e8fcb64e57b805955
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
categoryReason: Modernizing API usage from older, less efficient patterns to more direct and performant alternatives by using static parsing methods.
summary: This commit replaces `Boolean.valueOf(String).booleanValue()` with `Boolean.parseBoolean(String)`. This is a direct, semantically equivalent replacement that avoids an unnecessary `Boolean` object creation and an extra method call, improving performance and readability. The pattern is highly reusable and directly implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. `Boolean.parseBoolean()` exists since Java 1.5, so no `sourceVersionGE` guard is needed beyond the Java 8 baseline.

commitMessage:
Use switch over strings where possible

Change cascades of ifs which can be converted to switch over Strings. A
switch statement might be faster than an if-then-else chain. And it
improves clarity. The problem with if..else chain is that I have to look
into all the if conditions to understand what the program is doing. And
the variable might change in the chain processing.

Change-Id: I589f75d02e2fbe2567f7371a6cb1001e349bfd6a
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
categoryReason: The change transforms an 'if-else if' chain into a 'switch' statement, which is a structural change to control flow logic.
languageChangeNeeded: The TriggerPattern DSL primarily supports expression and simple statement replacements. Transforming an 'if-else if' chain into a 'switch' statement requires advanced structural AST manipulation (e.g., creating a new SwitchStatement node, migrating conditions to case labels, and handling default/fallthrough behavior) which is not currently supported.
summary: This commit replaces 'if-else if' cascades with 'switch' statements over strings or characters. This improves code readability and potentially performance. However, this is a structural transformation of control flow, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL. Implementing this would require significant DSL extensions for AST-level structural rewriting.

commitMessage:
Unnecessary temporary during conversion to String

Change-Id: I2e7616f92b63de296b9f46cf7365598106809408
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
categoryReason: Optimizing primitive-to-String conversion by avoiding unnecessary boxing and unboxing.
summary: This commit replaces `Integer.valueOf(int).toString()` with `Integer.toString(int)`. This change avoids the creation of an unnecessary `Integer` object for a simple primitive-to-string conversion, improving performance and being more direct. It is a highly reusable and directly implementable GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. `Integer.toString(int)` has been available since Java 1.0, so no `sourceVersionGE` guard is required.

commitMessage:
Bug 562091 - Unnecessary array removal quickfix

Simplified version of what happens when you run
"Remove unnecessary array creation for varargs"
on HierarchicalASTVisitorTest.java

Change-Id: Ic9a860a757c66cbafd61f00063659fffbfe9c1a1
Signed-off-by: Fabrice Tiercelin <fabrice.tiercelin@yahoo.fr>
categoryReason: The change involves modernizing collection API usage.
summary: The commit modernizes the `Collection.toArray()` usage from a pre-sized array (`new T[collection.size()]`) to a zero-length array (`new T[0]`). This is a standard Java 8+ idiom for minor performance and clarity. This specific transformation pattern is already covered by an existing DSL rule in `collection-toarray.sandbox-hint` and was previously proposed. The change in the `getPreview()` method is internal to the cleanup's functionality and not a user-facing code transformation pattern suitable for a DSL rule.

commitMessage:
Use multicatch

Change-Id: I707f32a87d5f158c9739b1030790cd631254e41b
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
categoryReason: The change modifies control flow structures by merging catch blocks.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support pattern matching and rewriting of `CatchClause` AST nodes, specifically merging multiple `CatchClause`s into one `CatchClause` with a `UnionType` (for the exception type) and potentially merging their bodies if they are identical. This is a significant structural AST manipulation beyond current expression-level replacement capabilities.
summary: The commit replaces separate `catch` blocks for `InterruptedException` and `IllegalArgumentException` with a single multi-catch block. This is a Java 7 feature that improves code readability and reduces boilerplate. However, implementing this as a TriggerPattern DSL rule requires advanced structural transformations of AST `CatchClause` nodes to merge them, which is beyond the current capabilities of the DSL. Therefore, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Use jdk 5 for-each loop

Replace simple uses of Iterator with a corresponding for-loop.

Change-Id: Ied3544f45c87710885a2d4859d35880438d64d05
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
categoryReason: The changes involve modernizing loop constructs and collection operations related to control flow.
languageChangeNeeded:
This commit contains two primary transformation patterns, both requiring significant DSL enhancements:
1.  **Indexed for-loop to enhanced for-loop**: Requires pattern matching a `ForStatement` and transforming it into an `EnhancedForStatement`, including extracting the loop variable's type and name, the collection being iterated, and safely adapting the loop body to use the new variable. This is a complex structural rewrite.
2.  **Explicit loop `add` to `addAll`**: Requires pattern matching an `EnhancedForStatement` (or `ForStatement`) whose body consists solely of an `.add()` call to a collection, and replacing the entire loop statement with a single `.$targetCollection.addAll($sourceCollection)` method invocation. This is also a complex structural rewrite involving control flow elimination and method call creation.
summary: The commit replaces traditional indexed `for` loops iterating over `List`s with Java 5 enhanced for-loops, and replaces loops that add elements one by one to a collection with a single `addAll()` call. Both transformations significantly improve readability and conciseness. However, they both require complex structural changes to AST nodes (converting a `ForStatement` to an `EnhancedForStatement`, or an entire loop statement into a single method call statement). These transformations are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, classifying this commit as RED. The `sandbox_functional_converter` plugin aims for similar loop transformations.

commitMessage:
Remove unnecessary array creation for varargs

Change-Id: I62e7ddabaa0d6cb13373a2855bcf44e73ece395a
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
irrelevantReason: The commit message 'Remove unnecessary array creation for varargs' suggests a code transformation. However, the actual diff provided only shows changes in whitespace and line breaks within existing `MessageFormat.format` calls. It does not show any `new Object[] { ... }` being removed or any other semantic code change that could be generalized into a DSL rule. The transformation described in the commit message is a type of cleanup, but the provided diff lines do not exhibit the pattern for mining.
categoryReason: The observed changes are purely stylistic, related to code formatting rather than semantic transformation. A category 'Code Formatting' is fitting for such changes.
summary: This commit is primarily a formatting change, adjusting line breaks and indentation for `MessageFormat.format` calls. Although the commit message indicates a cleanup to 'remove unnecessary array creation for varargs', the provided diff lines do not show `new Object[]{...}` being explicitly removed. Therefore, no concrete transformation pattern suitable for a DSL rule can be mined from the actual code changes presented.

commitMessage:
Bug 562501 - [14] Creating a Record which implements an interface does
not add interface methods

Change-Id: I745bd6b74ca7af5178dc4d959275279d63a746e2
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: The commit introduces new UI elements and backend logic within a JDT wizard (`NewRecordWizardPage`). This involves adding new fields, methods, NLS messages, and control flow for a new feature. This is an internal JDT UI feature enhancement.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL primarily supports expression-level transformations and annotation changes, not the introduction of new UI elements, fields, methods, or complex control flow logic for wizard functionality. Generating new NLS keys and their usage is also beyond the current DSL capabilities.
summary: This commit implements a new feature in the JDT 'New Record Wizard' to allow users to generate inherited interface methods. It involves adding new NLS message constants, introducing new UI controls (`SelectionButtonDialogFieldGroup`), and integrating new logic (methods like `setMethodStubSelection`, `isCreateInherited`) into the wizard page. The modification to `createTypeMembers` is a small part of a larger feature addition, where an argument is now derived from the new UI state. Overall, the changes are structural additions of new code and complex feature logic, which is beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based transformations.

commitMessage:
Bug 37872 - [quick fix] Add a constructor parameter

- Add constructor parameter and assign it to final field. This only
works for single constructor.
- Fix case for already initialized field in other constructor.
- Add test cases

Change-Id: Iebc73a30880806ff0e8520d541b46fba36f9b90f
Signed-off-by: Kenneth Styrberg <kenneth@kean.nu>
categoryReason: The commit introduces a new quick-fix feature in Eclipse JDT, modifying internal IDE logic to generate new code structures (method parameters, field assignments) based on complex AST analysis (e.g., finding constructors, suggesting names, determining insertion points). This is a change to the quick-fix's implementation, not a generalizable transformation on user code.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires extensive DSL extensions for structural code generation, complex AST analysis (e.g., finding a single constructor, suggesting names, calculating dynamic insertion points for statements), and cross-method binding analysis. The current DSL is focused on pattern matching and rewriting existing expressions or simple declarations, not generating complex method bodies or modifying control flow.
summary: This commit implements a new quick-fix feature in Eclipse JDT to add a constructor parameter and assign it to an uninitialized final field. The changes involve modifying internal JDT UI classes (`InitializeFinalFieldProposal`, `UnInitializedFinalFieldSubProcessor`) to introduce new quick-fix logic, update constructor signatures, and perform complex AST manipulations (e.g., creating new method parameters, generating assignment statements, inserting them into specific locations within method bodies). These are highly programmatic changes to the quick-fix's internal implementation and require advanced AST generation and semantic analysis capabilities that are not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Cleanup - Get rid of some warnings in build

see https://download.eclipse.org/eclipse/downloads/drops4/I20200421-1800/compilelogs/plugins/org.eclipse.jdt.ui_3.21.100.v20200421-1135/@dot.html#INFO_WARNINGS

Change-Id: Ia61dbb418d4d42302eec8d6d0e9254ce0bc232e1
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
categoryReason: The change involves removing @SuppressWarnings annotations, which is a common cleanup pattern for code style and warning suppression management.
languageChangeNeeded: While the syntactic removal of the @SuppressWarnings annotation is directly implementable, reliably determining if the annotation is 'unnecessary' (i.e., if the underlying warning is no longer present or was a false positive) requires full semantic analysis of the code, which is not available as a direct guard in the current DSL. A custom guard function (if it could execute Java code) would be needed for a fully safe and automatic removal. The addition of `//$NON-NLS-1$` comments is a change to line comments, not AST nodes, and is not expressible in the DSL.
summary: This commit removes `@SuppressWarnings("unused")` annotations from method parameters and methods, and `@SuppressWarnings("nls")` from a method. It also adds `//$NON-NLS-1$` comments to string literals. The removal of these `@SuppressWarnings` annotations is a common code cleanup for warning management. While the syntactic removal of annotations can be expressed in the DSL, determining whether they are truly 'unnecessary' requires semantic analysis (e.g., checking if the warning they suppress is actually present or not), which is beyond the current DSL's direct capabilities for guards. Therefore, this is classified as YELLOW. The addition of NLS comments is a change to code comments, not an AST transformation, and is not supported by the DSL.
dslValidationResult: Parse error at line 2: Missing replacement after '=>' (line 2)
